profile.background: U.S. Representative AZ-07 (Tucson + SE Arizona), sworn 11/12/2025. Daughter of late Rep. Raúl Grijalva (D, died 3/13/2025). 20 years on Tucson Unified School District board. Pima County Supervisor 2020-2025 (Board Chair). Won 7/15/2025 D primary, won 9/23/2025 special general 68.9-29.4 over Daniel Butierez (R). claims.text: As Pima County Board of Supervisors Chair, led the county's formal rebuttal of the Arizona Supreme Court's 1864 abortion-ban ruling and directed county resources to preserve abortion access after Dobbs. Won her 2025 House special election with the endorsement of Reproductive Freedom for All as a champion for unrestricted abortion access — rejecting any life-at-conception standard.

In her first week sworn in (November 2025), Grijalva cosponsored 56 bills including the Gun Violence Prevention Research Act and Ethan's Law (mandating safe-storage of firearms in homes with children) — joining gun-restriction legislation that opposes constitutional carry and unrestricted Second Amendment rights.

Voted against the FY2026 DHS Funding Bill that expanded ICE funding, calling ICE 'corrupted to the core' and opposing mandatory deportation; co-introduced the Fund Schools Not ICE Act (July 2026) to redirect $38.5 billion in ICE reconciliation funding to Title I public schools and require DHS to sell 11 newly-purchased detention facilities — directly opposing the rubric's support for mandatory deportation of illegal immigrants.

candidates.name: Alexander Kolodin
candidates.office: U.S. Representative AZ-01 (NOT RUNNING — pivoted to AZ Secretary of State 2026; endorsed Joseph Chaplik for AZ-01)
candidates.notes: AZ State Rep LD-3 (R) since Jan 2023; AZ Freedom Caucus; Chair Select Committee on Election Integrity. CANDIDACY: Announced AZ Secretary of State campaign Mar 31, 2025; NOT running for AZ-01 (which the existing scorecard record incorrectly placed him in). [2026-06 federal autopilot — scored on AZ state-rep record at federal-tier rubric] HARD evidence wins (state record applied to federal-tier rubric): Sponsored HB 2681 (in-person exam requirement for chemical abortion); YES SB 1600 born-alive infant protection; YES HB 2547 prohibiting state funds to abortion providers; NO HB 2677 (2024 repeal of 1864 abortion ban — voted to keep total ban); signed 2024 AZ GOP platform minority dissent against softening on abortion ('We're willing to kill infants to win an election... it's not worth our souls'; right-to-life-from-conception language); on-record marriage-as-man-woman position; supported 2025 ban on birth-certificate gender changes; KEY DRAFTER of Prop 314 (Secure the Border Act 2024); sponsored HCR 2001 (2026) Secure Elections Act requiring voter-ID and banning foreign election money; sponsored HB 2785 (2024) election deadline reconciliation w/ federal ECRA; sponsored 'Shall Not Be Infringed Act' (2025) repealing AZ machine gun/silencer/SBR/pipe-bomb bans; 2020 Kraken-adjacent litigation. NO HARD EVIDENCE FOUND on foreign-policy-restraint, industry-capture, federal CBDC/BBA positions — left null (state rep with no federal exposure). Religion: Jewish but campaign bio uses Christian-liberty framing.

profile.background: Arizona State Representative (LD-3 Scottsdale) since Jan 9, 2023. Chair of Select Committee on Election Integrity. Member: Federalism/Military Affairs/Elections; Judiciary; Public Safety. Member of Arizona Freedom Caucus. Georgetown undergrad; UPenn Law. Former Reagan Fellow at Goldwater Institute's Scharf Norton Center for Constitutional Litigation. PIVOTED OFFICES MAR 31 2025: NOT running for AZ-01 U.S. House — running for ARIZONA SECRETARY OF STATE in 2026 (R primary July 21, 2026); Apr 2026 NextGen poll: leads AZ GOP Chair Gina Swoboda by 9 pts. Endorsed former Rep. Joseph Chaplik for AZ-01 instead. 2020 Kraken-adjacent election lawyer for Trump campaign; State Bar of Arizona sanctioned/18-mo probation Dec 2023, ~$2,700 court costs. Husband, father of two; lives in Scottsdale.

claims.text: In the Arizona House (2023-2026), Kolodin voted to prohibit state government bodies from contracting with or funding entities that provide or promote abortions, and voted to require physicians to perform in-person exams and give patients a right to sue manufacturers before abortion medication is prescribed or shipped — a consistent pro-life legislative record across multiple bill cycles.

Sponsored HCR2037 'Shall Not Be Infringed Act' (57th Legislature, 1st regular session, 2025), which passed the Arizona House 32-27. The resolution proposes a ballot measure to decriminalize NFA-restricted weapons (machine guns, suppressors, short-barreled rifles/shotguns) in Arizona and to prohibit state law-enforcement from participating in federal NFA enforcement actions — the most aggressive state-level Second Amendment nullification bill in recent Arizona history.

As chair of the Arizona House Select Committee on Election Integrity, advanced a state-level SAVE Act clone requiring documentary proof of citizenship for voter registration, restrictions on no-excuse mail-in voting, a shortened early-voting window, and a return to smaller precinct-based in-person voting — the most sweeping election-integrity overhaul proposed in Arizona in the 2025-2026 session.
